lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Welcome to Money Matters, a weekly program that helps you manage your money.
lines.translation: 欢迎收看《理财至上》，这是一个每周一次的、帮助您打理钱财的节目。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Tonight, I'll be talking to Mary Johnson about budgeting.
lines.translation: 今晚，我将与玛丽·约翰逊谈论预算的话题。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Hello everyone.
lines.translation: 大家好。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: There's a magic about money.
lines.translation: 金钱有一种魔力。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: When it's not planned for, tracked, and kept a record of, it literally disappears.
lines.translation: 如果没有计划、追踪和记录，它真的会消失。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: What are some of the steps we can take to prevent this from happening?
lines.translation: 我们可以采取哪些措施来防止这种情况发生？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: It's all about keeping track of your money.
lines.translation: 关键在于追踪你的资金流向。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: If you don't do that, you'll never be able to set any goals for your budget or have the discipline to stick to them.
lines.translation: 如果你不这样做，你将永远无法为你的预算设定任何目标，也没有纪律去坚持执行。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: That's easier said than done.
lines.translation: 说起来容易做起来难。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I read recently that only 41% of Americans adhere to a budget.
lines.translation: 我最近读到，只有41%的美国人坚持执行预算。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Yes, but knowing what you earn and what you spend can give you reassurance that you won't get into debt in the first place.
lines.translation: 是的，但是了解你的收入和支出可以让你确信，你首先不会陷入债务。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: You can do this by adding up all of your sources of income you have and writing them all down on a piece of paper.
lines.translation: 你可以通过把你所有的收入来源加起来，并把它们全部写在一张纸上来做到这一点。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: On the same page, write down all of your monthly expenses.
lines.translation: 在同一页上，写下你所有的每月支出。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I'm always amazed at how much my expenses add up, but designating each item as an income or an expense really helps me have a much better sense of all my spending.
lines.translation: 我总是惊讶于我的开支加起来有多少，但是将每一项指定为收入或支出，确实有助于我更好地了解我所有的消费。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Right.
lines.translation: 没错。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Most people have no idea how much they spend each day, let alone each week or month, no matter how careful they are.
lines.translation: 大多数人不知道他们每天花多少钱，更不用说每周或每月了，无论他们多么小心。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Next, subtract your monthly expenses from income.
lines.translation: 接下来，用收入减去每月的开支。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: If the result is positive, you are living within your means.
lines.translation: 如果结果是正数，说明你的生活量入为出。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: If the result is a negative number, you're going to have to cut back on your spending.
lines.translation: 如果结果是负数，你就必须削减开支了。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I'm usually a negative number.
lines.translation: 我通常是负数。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I just can't resist the lure of all those prestigious goods.
lines.translation: 我就是无法抗拒那些名牌商品的诱惑。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Well, it's not a catastrophe.
lines.translation: 嗯，这还不算是一场灾难。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: But you do have to make some changes.
lines.translation: 但你确实需要做出一些改变。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Try cutting back on those non-essential items, sell some stuff in your attic or shop online to avoid unnecessary temptations like chocolate.
lines.translation: 尝试削减那些非必需品，卖掉阁楼里的一些东西，或者在线购物以避免巧克力等不必要的诱惑。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Failing that you can always find yourself a part-time job.
lines.translation: 如果做不到这些，你总是可以找一份兼职工作。

lines.speaker: Q1.
lines.text: What does the man say about the weekly program?
lines.translation: 关于这个每周一次的节目，男士说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q2.
lines.text: What did the man read recently?
lines.translation: 男士最近读到了什么？

lines.speaker: Q3.
lines.text: What does the woman suggest the man do first to avoid getting into debt?
lines.translation: 女士建议男士首先做什么以避免陷入债务？

lines.speaker: Q4.
lines.text: What does the woman say about online shopping?
lines.translation: 女士关于在线购物说了什么？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Hi there.
lines.translation: 你好。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I've come to see the flat.
lines.translation: 我是来看公寓的。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: My name is Mark Adams.
lines.translation: 我叫马克·亚当斯。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: We spoke on the phone on Wednesday.
lines.translation: 我们周三在电话里聊过。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Hi Mark.
lines.translation: 嗨，马克。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Come on up.
lines.translation: 上来吧。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I'll buzz you in.
lines.translation: 我给你开门。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Green door on the second floor, on the right side.
lines.translation: 二楼右侧的绿门。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Nice to meet you.
lines.translation: 见到你很高兴。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I spoke to all your references and they all checked out.
lines.translation: 我联系了你所有的推荐人，他们都没问题。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Okay?
lines.translation: 好吗？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: So let me show you around.
lines.translation: 那我带你四处看看吧。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: The place actually belongs to my mother, but her health isn't great.
lines.translation: 这个地方实际上是我母亲的，但她的健康状况不太好。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: We finally managed to persuade her to move in with us and rent this old place out.
lines.translation: 我们终于说服她搬来和我们一起住，并把这个旧地方租出去。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: It's a great size, plenty of space, very versatile.
lines.translation: 面积很大，空间充裕，用途广泛。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I think it's a winner for us.
lines.translation: 我想这对我们来说很合适。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Yes.
lines.translation: 是的。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: All the appliances are brand new.
lines.translation: 所有的家电都是崭新的。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: There's a washing machine and a tumble dryer in the utility room next to the kitchen.
lines.translation: 厨房旁边的杂物间里有一台洗衣机和一台烘干机。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Lots of closet space, too, which is fabulous.
lines.translation: 还有很多衣柜空间，这太棒了。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: My wife has a ridiculous number of shoes.
lines.translation: 我妻子的鞋多得离谱。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Now, the big question, what about noise and the neighbors?
lines.translation: 现在，大问题来了，噪音和邻居的情况怎么样？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: All the neighbors are elderly, so no noisy kids.
lines.translation: 所有的邻居都是老人，所以没有吵闹的孩子。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: And the back of the house overlooks a clear and peaceful pond.
lines.translation: 房子背面俯瞰着一个清澈平静的池塘。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: It's perfect if tranquility is what you are looking for.
lines.translation: 如果你追求安宁，这里再合适不过了。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: That's good news.
lines.translation: 那真是个好消息。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: We've been living in a less than glamorous part of Aberdeen, constantly harassed day and night by noisy neighbors.
lines.translation: 我们一直住在阿伯丁一个不太体面的地方，日夜受到吵闹邻居的骚扰。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Getting to work was a nightmare, too.
lines.translation: 上班也是一场噩梦。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: As we only have one car, and my wife has to use it as she works nights at the hospital.
lines.translation: 因为我们只有一辆车，而且我妻子在医院上夜班，她必须用它。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: If you like the place, it's yours as soon as I get a contract drawn up with the solicitor.
lines.translation: 如果你喜欢这里，只要我和律师起草好合同，它就是你的了。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: The first month's rent and a deposit are mandatory on signing the contract, then we can work out when is the best day for you to pay rent each month.
lines.translation: 签订合同时必须支付第一个月的租金和押金，然后我们可以商定每月支付租金的最佳日期。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: We'll be incredibly happy to be your new tenants.
lines.translation: 能成为你的新房客，我们会非常高兴。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Thank you so much.
lines.translation: 非常感谢。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: My wife will be thrilled to get out of the shabby place we are now in and start filling those wardrobes with all those shoes.
lines.translation: 我妻子会很高兴离开我们现在住的破旧地方，并开始把那些衣柜塞满她所有的鞋子。

lines.speaker: Q5.
lines.text: What does the woman say about the flat?
lines.translation: 女士关于公寓说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q6.
lines.text: What is the man's chief consideration in looking for a flat?
lines.translation: 男士寻找公寓的主要考虑因素是什么？

lines.speaker: Q7.
lines.text: What does the man have to do on signing the contract?
lines.translation: 男士在签订合同时必须做什么？

lines.speaker: Q8.
lines.text: Why does the man say his wife will feel very excited if they move into the flat?
lines.translation: 为什么男士说如果他们搬进公寓，他妻子会感到非常激动？

lines.text: A new study has found a positive correlation between how much television children watch and their parents' stress levels.
lines.translation: 一项新研究发现，儿童看电视的时长与父母的压力水平呈正相关。

lines.text: Why?
lines.translation: 为什么？

lines.text: Because the more television kids watch, the more they're exposed to advertising.
lines.translation: 因为孩子看电视越多，接触广告就越多。

lines.text: The more advertising they see, the more likely they are to insist on purchasing items when they go with their parents to the store.
lines.translation: 他们看到的广告越多，当他们和父母去商店时，就越有可能坚持购买商品。

lines.text: This could generate conflict if the parents refuse.
lines.translation: 如果父母拒绝，这可能会产生冲突。

lines.text: All that, researchers say, can contribute to parents' overall stress levels.
lines.translation: 研究人员表示，所有这些都会增加父母的整体压力水平。

lines.text: What's the solution?
lines.translation: 解决办法是什么？

lines.text: Perhaps the most obvious is curtailing screen time.
lines.translation: 也许最明显的方法是缩短屏幕时间。

lines.text: Commercial content is there for a reason: to elicit purchasing behavior.
lines.translation: 商业内容的存在是有原因的：为了诱发购买行为。

lines.text: So, parents might want to shut off the TV.
lines.translation: 因此，父母可能想关掉电视。

lines.text: Researchers concede that this is easier said than done, so they suggest another option.
lines.translation: 研究人员承认这说起来容易做起来难，所以他们建议了另一个选择。

lines.text: Parents can change how they talk to their kids about purchases.
lines.translation: 父母可以改变他们与孩子谈论购买行为的方式。

lines.text: The researchers suggest that parents seek input from their children on family purchasing decisions.
lines.translation: 研究人员建议，父母在做出家庭购买决策时征求孩子的意见。

lines.text: They shouldn't try to control all purchases.
lines.translation: 他们不应该试图控制所有的购买。

lines.text: Instead, parents might tell their children things like, "I will listen to your advice on certain products or brands."
lines.translation: 相反，父母可以告诉孩子，比如：“我会听取你对某些产品或品牌的建议。”

lines.text: This type of communication, the researchers assert, can lead to children making fewer purchasing demands.
lines.translation: 研究人员断言，这种类型的沟通可以减少孩子的购买要求。

lines.text: That means less parents' stress.
lines.translation: 这意味着父母的压力更小。

lines.text: However, the protective effect of this kind of communication diminishes with greater exposure to television.
lines.translation: 然而，这种沟通的保护作用会随着看电视时间的增加而减弱。

lines.text: This is because advertising aimed at children is especially persuasive.
lines.translation: 这是因为针对儿童的广告特别具有说服力。

lines.text: Advertisers use an assortment of tactics, such as bright colors, happy music, and celebrity endorsements to appeal to children.
lines.translation: 广告商使用各种策略，如明亮的色彩、欢乐的音乐和名人代言来吸引儿童。

lines.text: Plus, children don't have the cognitive ability to fully understand advertising's intent, which makes them particularly vulnerable to advertisements.
lines.translation: 此外，儿童不具备充分理解广告意图的认知能力，这使得他们特别容易受到广告的影响。

lines.speaker: Q9.
lines.text: What has the new study found about children watching television?
lines.translation: 关于儿童看电视，这项新研究发现了什么？

lines.speaker: Q10.
lines.text: What are parents advised to do to reduce the impact of TV commercials?
lines.translation: 父母被建议如何做以减少电视广告的影响？

lines.speaker: Q11.
lines.text: What makes children particularly vulnerable to TV commercials?
lines.translation: 是什么让儿童特别容易受到电视广告的影响？

lines.text: Everyone is supposed to cheer for good guys.
lines.translation: 每个人都应该为好人欢呼。

lines.text: We should only punish the bad guys.
lines.translation: 我们应该只惩罚坏人。

lines.text: That's not what we always do.
lines.translation: 但这并不总是我们所做的。

lines.text: Most of the time, we do indeed reward good people.
lines.translation: 大多数时候，我们确实奖励好人。

lines.text: We also often punish people who harm others, or who aren't good team players.
lines.translation: 我们也经常惩罚那些伤害他人或不具备良好团队合作精神的人。

lines.text: But sometimes the good guys also get punished or criticized, specifically because they are so good.
lines.translation: 但有时好人也会受到惩罚或批评，正因为他们太优秀了。

lines.text: This seems baffling because it's detrimental to group cooperation.
lines.translation: 这似乎令人费解，因为它不利于团队合作。

lines.text: However, the phenomenon has been discovered in multiple fields.
lines.translation: 然而，这一现象已在多个领域被发现。

lines.text: It has been found in every society.
lines.translation: 在每一个社会中都发现了这种现象。

lines.text: Why does this happen?
lines.translation: 为什么会发生这种事？

lines.text: Research suggests a simple reason.
lines.translation: 研究提出了一个简单的原因。

lines.text: When one person looks really good, others look bad by comparison.
lines.translation: 当一个人看起来非常优秀时，其他人相比之下就显得糟糕了。

lines.text: Those others then have an incentive in stopping that person from looking good, especially if they can't or won't compete.
lines.translation: 于是，其他人就有动机阻止那个人表现得优秀，特别是如果他们不能或不愿竞争的话。

lines.text: After all, we're all judged in comparison with others.
lines.translation: 毕竟，我们都是在与他人的比较中被评判的。

lines.text: When faced with someone better, what can a normal person do?
lines.translation: 面对比自己更好的人，一个普通人能做什么？

lines.text: One option is to actively compete.
lines.translation: 一种选择是积极竞争。

lines.text: A second option is to bring that person down.
lines.translation: 第二种选择是把那个人拉下水。

lines.text: That is to suppress their cooperation or work ethic, infer selfish motives for their actions, or imply real or imagined hypocrisy.
lines.translation: 也就是抑制他们的合作或职业道德，推测他们行为背后的自私动机，或者暗示真实或虚构的虚伪。

lines.text: Other tactics include attacking them on unrelated dimensions or punishing them outright.
lines.translation: 其他策略包括在无关维度上攻击他们，或直接惩罚他们。

lines.text: Why does this matter?
lines.translation: 这为什么重要？

lines.text: Critics often attack the motives of people who protect the environment, donate money, or work too hard.
lines.translation: 批评者经常攻击那些保护环境、捐款或工作过于努力的人的动机。

lines.text: Such good deeds are dismissed as naive or hypocritical by those who do not perform those deeds.
lines.translation: 这些善举被那些不履行这些善举的人斥为天真或虚伪。

lines.text: This criticism may ultimately discourage people from doing good deeds.
lines.translation: 这种批评最终可能会阻碍人们做善事。

lines.text: So it's important to recognize these attacks for what they are.
lines.translation: 因此，认清这些攻击的本质是很重要的。

lines.speaker: Q12.
lines.text: What baffling phenomenon is discussed in the passage?
lines.translation: 文章中讨论了什么令人费解的现象？

lines.speaker: Q13.
lines.text: How are we all judged according to the passage?
lines.translation: 根据文章，我们都是如何被评判的？

lines.speaker: Q14.
lines.text: What can a normal person do when faced with people who perform better?
lines.translation: 当面对表现更好的人时，普通人会怎么做？

lines.speaker: Q15.
lines.text: What may discourage people from performing good deeds?
lines.translation: 什么可能会阻碍人们行善？

lines.text: In America, most researchers concede that boys and girls are brought up in different ways, taught different skills and rewarded for different acts.
lines.translation: 在美国，大多数研究人员承认，男孩和女孩的抚养方式不同，教给他们的技能不同，对不同行为的奖励也不同。

lines.text: Women, it is agreed, excel at certain tasks, men at others.
lines.translation: 人们一致认为，女性擅长某些任务，而男性擅长其他任务。

lines.text: There is little argument that some personality traits appear more dominant in one sex than in the other.
lines.translation: 几乎没有人怀疑某些性格特征在一种性别中比在另一种性别中表现得更明显。

lines.text: All of this notwithstanding, gender differences are very much in the media these days.
lines.translation: 尽管如此，性别差异在当今媒体上非常受关注。

lines.text: Since the rise of the women's movement, gender role behavior has come under closer scrutiny.
lines.translation: 自妇女运动兴起以来，性别角色行为受到了更严密的审视。

lines.text: How has this affected friendship?
lines.translation: 这如何影响了友谊？

lines.text: How do the sexes differ in their friendship relations?
lines.translation: 不同性别在友谊关系上有何不同？

lines.text: Most pre-teen children have a best friend who is usually someone of the same sex and similar age.
lines.translation: 大多数青春期前的儿童都有一个最好的朋友，通常是性别相同、年龄相仿的人。

lines.text: Both sexes share an essentially positive recollection of these childhood friendships.
lines.translation: 两性对这些童年友谊都有着基本正面的回忆。

lines.text: They do not differ in this respect.
lines.translation: 在这方面他们没有区别。

lines.text: However, the type of play engaged in during these early friendships is telling of the difference to come.
lines.translation: 然而，在早期友谊中参与的游戏类型预示了未来的差异。

lines.text: Boys tend to form play groups that are competitive in nature.
lines.translation: 男孩倾向于形成具有竞争性质的游戏群体。

lines.text: Girls' groups more frequently revolve around cooperative enterprises.
lines.translation: 女孩的群体则更多地围绕合作性的活动展开。

lines.text: Thus at an early age, boys become concerned with trying hard and winning, while girls, by contrast, play house and school, engaging in roles that require complementary support.
lines.translation: 因此，在很小的时候，男孩就开始关注努力和获胜，而相比之下，女孩玩过家家和学校游戏，扮演需要互补支持的角色。

lines.text: Men recall being highly responsive to and aware of the gender role opinions of other boys.
lines.translation: 男性回忆起他们对其他男孩的性别角色看法非常敏感且关注。

lines.text: Girls in pre-teen years appear to be less susceptible to gender role pressure.
lines.translation: 青春期前的女孩似乎不太容易受到性别角色压力的影响。

lines.text: It is not until the dating years that women report being concerned with feminine behavior.
lines.translation: 直到约会年龄，女性才表示开始关注女性化行为。

lines.text: Males, for the most part, are responsive to the suggestion that their behavior is unmanly at almost any age.
lines.translation: 在大多数情况下，男性几乎在任何年龄都会对他们的行为不具男子气概的暗示做出反应。

lines.text: These early attitudes reinforced by social conditioning continue to play an active part in the friendships of both sexes during adolescence.
lines.translation: 这些由社会环境强化的早期态度在青春期两性的友谊中继续发挥着积极作用。

lines.text: This is a period when the majority of males, once again, report a close alliance with same-sex friends.
lines.translation: 这是一个大多数男性再次表示与同性朋友结成紧密联盟的时期。

lines.text: Now, however, with heightened intensity, considerable energy is devoted to competing for position, and a definite undercurrent of competition permeates the relationship.
lines.translation: 然而，现在的强度增加了，大量的精力投入到地位的竞争中，一段明确的竞争潜流渗透在关系中。

lines.text: Although in dissimilar fashion, females share equally fragile relationships at this age.
lines.translation: 虽然方式不同，但女性在这个年龄也拥有同样脆弱的关系。

lines.text: For them, the bond of loyalty extends only to the line of romantic involvement.
lines.translation: 对她们来说，忠诚的纽带只延伸到浪漫恋情的界限。

lines.text: This is most apt to be the case in late adolescence, when dating and relationships with boys take sharp precedence over sisterhood.
lines.translation: 这在青春期后期最为常见，当时约会和与男孩的关系明显优先于姐妹情谊。

lines.text: Actually, dating dilutes the intensity of same-sex friendships for men also.
lines.translation: 事实上，约会也会冲淡男性之间同性友谊的强度。

lines.text: For the majority of us, the moment we begin to date seriously, there's a competition between romance and friendship.
lines.translation: 对于我们大多数人来说，当我们开始认真约会的那一刻，浪漫和友谊之间就存在着竞争。

lines.speaker: Q16.
lines.text: What does the speaker say about most pre-teen children?
lines.translation: 演讲者关于大多数青春期前的儿童说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q17.
lines.text: What do most males devote much of their energy to during adolescence?
lines.translation: 青春期大多数男性把大部分精力投入到什么事情上？

lines.speaker: Q18.
lines.text: What do children do when they reach late adolescence?
lines.translation: 孩子们到了青春期后期会做什么？

lines.text: Good afternoon.
lines.translation: 下午好。

lines.text: Today's lecture, we'll be talking about how and when to disclose a disability when applying for a job.
lines.translation: 在今天的讲座中，我们将讨论在申请工作时如何以及何时披露残疾情况。

lines.text: On average, about 20% of the population has some form of disability.
lines.translation: 平均而言，约20%的人口患有某种形式的残疾。

lines.text: Most countries these days have equal opportunity and nondiscrimination laws.
lines.translation: 现在大多数国家都有平等机会和非歧视法律。

lines.text: Yet, disabled people often find it hard to decide when, how, and if at all, to raise their disability problem with a potential employer.
lines.translation: 然而，残疾人往往很难决定何时、如何，以及是否向潜在雇主提出他们的残疾问题。

lines.text: There is uncertainty about how a recruiter will perceive their disability.
lines.translation: 招聘人员如何看待他们的残疾存在不确定性。

lines.text: As such, many candidates fear they wouldn't be considered for a position as a result of disclosing this personal information.
lines.translation: 因此，许多候选人担心由于披露了这一个人信息，他们将不会被考虑录用。

lines.text: And research has validated this as a genuine concern for many job applicants.
lines.translation: 研究已经证实，这是许多求职者真正的担忧。

lines.text: It's a natural reaction, but it shouldn't be a reason to stay quiet.
lines.translation: 这是一种自然反应，但不应该成为保持沉默的理由。

lines.text: People need to remember that they are applying for a position they have the skills and experience to excel in.
lines.translation: 人们需要记住，他们正在申请一个他们拥有技能和经验来胜任的职位。

lines.text: Discussing a disability with a potential employer may help them make reasonable workplace adjustments in their favor.
lines.translation: 与潜在雇主讨论残疾问题可能有助于他们做出有利于求职者的合理工作场所调整。

lines.text: It's most appropriate to discuss a disability when they reply to confirm an interview.
lines.translation: 在回复确认面试时讨论残疾问题是最合适的。

lines.text: This information needn't be put up front in their cover letter or resume, because it's probably not relevant to the position itself.
lines.translation: 这些信息不需要放在求职信或简历的最前面，因为它可能与职位本身无关。

lines.text: Candidates with disabilities should feel they have the power to make their own decisions around sharing this information free from prejudice.
lines.translation: 残疾应聘者应该觉得自己有权在不受偏见影响的情况下，就分享这些信息做出自己的决定。

lines.text: If they find an organization that doesn't celebrate diversity and inclusion, it could say a lot about the company's culture.
lines.translation: 如果他们发现一个不推崇多样性和包容性的组织，这能说明很多关于该公司文化的问题。

lines.text: Perhaps the organization isn't the right fit.
lines.translation: 也许这个组织并不合适。

lines.text: It's important for them to remember that they are seeking a manager and employer that's going to be supportive and continue to give them a great employment experience.
lines.translation: 对他们来说，记住他们正在寻找的是一位能够给予支持、并继续为他们提供良好就业体验的经理和雇主，这一点很重要。

lines.text: Companies sometimes offer candidates the chance to disclose disabilities on their application form, but people shouldn't feel restricted by this method or timing.
lines.translation: 公司有时会在申请表上为候选人提供披露残疾的机会，但人们不应受此方式或时间的限制。

lines.text: People should avoid sharing the name of their disability or condition.
lines.translation: 人们应该避免分享他们残疾或疾病的名称。

lines.text: There is always the risk that the recruiter will research information that is inaccurate or irrelevant.
lines.translation: 总存在这样的风险：招聘人员会去搜索不准确或无关的信息。

lines.text: If people don't think their disability will impact their ability to perform in the advertised position, then it's entirely their entitlement to choose when and whether to share this information.
lines.translation: 如果人们认为自己的残疾不会影响他们在所招聘职位上的表现，那么何时以及是否分享这些信息完全是他们的权利。

lines.speaker: Q19.
lines.text: Why do disabled job applicants feel reluctant to disclose their disability information?
lines.translation: 为什么残疾求职者不愿披露他们的残疾信息？

lines.speaker: Q20.
lines.text: When does the speaker suggest applicants reveal their disability information?
lines.translation: 演讲者建议申请人何时透露他们的残疾信息？

lines.speaker: Q21.
lines.text: What are people advised to do when filling out their job application form?
lines.translation: 人们在填写求职申请表时被建议怎么做？

lines.text: Smartphones distract attention and reduce learning because of their potential to offer activities more inviting than study.
lines.translation: 智能手机会分散注意力并降低学习效率，因为它们可能提供比学习更诱人的活动。

lines.text: But what about background sound alone?
lines.translation: 但是，仅仅背景声音又是怎样的呢？

lines.text: A group of American researchers compared students' comprehension of verbal material when reading in the presence of background speech, instrumental music or general noise.
lines.translation: 一组美国研究人员比较了学生在有背景对话、器乐或一般噪音的情况下阅读口头材料时的理解能力。

lines.text: General noise is neutral, such as that from the sound of an air conditioner or fan.
lines.translation: 一般噪音是中性的，例如来自空调或风扇的声音。

lines.text: Students' scores were most depressed in the presence of background speech.
lines.translation: 在背景对话存在的情况下，学生的成绩下降最为明显。

lines.text: Comprehension was slightly better with the presence of music than with speech.
lines.translation: 有音乐时的理解力略好于有对话时。

lines.text: However, when they were asked to identify melodies rather than understand text, background music interfered more.
lines.translation: 然而，当要求他们识别旋律而不是理解文本时，背景音乐的干扰更大。

lines.text: When the background speech was in a language unfamiliar to participants, there was little if any hindrance of reading comprehension.
lines.translation: 当背景对话使用的是参与者不熟悉的语言时，对阅读理解几乎没有任何阻碍。

lines.text: British researchers compared the effects of background speech, vocal music, instrumental music, general background noise, and silence on short-term memory.
lines.translation: 英国研究人员比较了背景对话、声乐、器乐、一般背景噪音和安静环境对短期记忆的影响。

lines.text: Background speech had the biggest negative effect.
lines.translation: 背景对话的负面影响最大。

lines.text: Vocal music was slightly more disruptive than instrumental.
lines.translation: 声乐比器乐更具干扰性。

lines.text: In general, background noise and silence were least disruptive.
lines.translation: 总的来说，背景噪音和安静环境的干扰最小。

lines.text: It seems the degree of interference from background noise depends on the overlap between the processing required on the task and the processing required to screen out the background noise.
lines.translation: 背景噪音的干扰程度似乎取决于任务所需的处理与屏蔽背景噪音所需的处理之间的重合度。

lines.text: The studies suggest that when people read and when they try to remember any verbal material, background speech will inhibit their ability.
lines.translation: 研究表明，当人们阅读并试图记住任何口头材料时，背景对话会抑制他们的能力。

lines.text: Instrumental music will have, at worst, a slight effect.
lines.translation: 器乐最坏的情况下也只有轻微的影响。

lines.text: When students write essays, however, other research has found it is best to reduce all background noise as much as possible.
lines.translation: 然而，其他研究发现，当学生写论文时，最好尽可能减少所有背景噪音。

lines.text: Not everyone reacts in the same way to distractions.
lines.translation: 并非每个人对干扰的反应都一样。

lines.text: Other studies suggest some aspects of personality may make a difference.
lines.translation: 其他研究表明，性格的某些方面可能会产生影响。

lines.text: The researchers subjected shy, quiet people and confident, outgoing ones to high arousal or low arousal background music, general noise or silence while asking them to remember words.
lines.translation: 研究人员让害羞安静的人和自信外向的人在处于高唤醒或低唤醒的背景音乐、一般噪音或安静环境中记住单词。

lines.text: Everyone performed best in the silent condition, but less sociable people were more negatively affected by each of the distractions.
lines.translation: 每个人在安静的环境下表现最好，但不善际的人受每种干扰的负面影响更大。

lines.text: So when children are reading and trying to incorporate new material, parents could consider allowing some background music, particularly if it is instrumental and their child is the outgoing type.
lines.translation: 因此，当孩子们在阅读并试图吸收新内容时，父母可以考虑允许一些背景音乐，特别是如果是器乐且孩子是外向型的话。

lines.speaker: Q22.
lines.text: What did the American researchers find about students' reading comprehension?
lines.translation: 美国研究人员关于学生的阅读理解发现了什么？

lines.speaker: Q23.
lines.text: What determines the degree of interference from background noise according to the passage?
lines.translation: 根据文章，什么决定了背景噪音的干扰程度？

lines.speaker: Q24.
lines.text: What do we learn about the effect of distractions on people of different personalities?
lines.translation: 关于干扰对不同性格的人的影响，我们学到了什么？

lines.speaker: Q25.
lines.text: What does the speaker suggest parents do when their children are reading?
lines.translation: 当孩子阅读时，演讲者建议父母做什么？
